title: Front-half sketchbook and partner phase
summary: Beach and coastal-scrub still have the lowest sketchbook note coverage and the fewest field partner cues in the live chain. The sketchbook half of this wave should start as one exact four-entry note pack before the packet moves on to the partner-cue expansion.

execution_order.why_now: Beach and coastal-scrub sketchbook coverage is half of forest's.
Sketchbook pages feel empty in beach and coastal-scrub.
Sketchbook notes set tone; they need quality review.

execution_order.goal: Prepare front-half field partner expansion handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Beach has only 3 partner cues — the thinnest of any biome.

execution_order.goal: Implement front-half field partner cue expansion.
execution_order.why_now: The field partner barely speaks in beach; it should have weather and zone cues.
Partner cues must feel like notebook margins, not tutorial chatter.

guardrails: Sketchbook notes should be poetic one-liners capturing the feel of the species in place.
The first sketchbook step should add exactly four notes: two beach entries and two coastal-scrub entries.
Use local front-half entries for the first note pack instead of reopening shared dune carriers.
Field partner cues must follow the existing cue bank structure: biome, zone, world-state keyed.
Partner cues must default to silence, not fill every state.
No new partner UI surface — stay on the existing transient strip.
